CSPC Pharmaceutical Group ( (HK:1093) ) just unveiled an update.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group has granted 46,462,500 restricted shares, equivalent to about 0.4% of its issued share capital, to 727 selected employees under its restricted share award scheme at nil consideration. The shares will vest in three equal tranches between January 2027 and January 2029, subject to individual performance targets over three consecutive financial years and a clawback mechanism tied to misconduct or regulatory breaches, underscoring the company’s use of equity incentives to retain and motivate staff while leaving 61,575,868 shares available for future grants under the scheme.

Genscript Biotech ( (HK:1548) ) has shared an update.

Genscript Biotech has announced that its chairman and executive director, Jiange (Robin) Meng, has been appointed to the board of its associate company Legend Biotech Corporation, effective 20 January 2026, replacing outgoing director Li Zhu with a term running until Legend’s 2026 annual general meeting. United Company RUSAL has announced the payment of coupon income on its BO-001P-12 series exchange-traded bonds, denominated in Chinese yuan and placed under its 001R exchange bond programme. For the tenth coupon period, covering 23 December 2025 to 22 January 2026, the company paid a total of RUB 64.71 million on 650,000 bonds, reflecting an annual coupon rate of 10.90%, equal to RUB 99.55 or approximately CNY 8.96 per bond, underscoring its continued servicing of debt obligations in the yuan market and its use of ruble settlements based on the Bank of Russia’s exchange rate.

Gemilang International Limited reported an increase in revenue to approximately US$25.97 million for the year ended 31 October 2025, up from about US$22.96 million a year earlier, driven primarily by higher sales of bus bodies and kits and growth in parts and related services. Yuexiu Transport Infrastructure ( (HK:1052) ) has provided an update.

Yuexiu Transport Infrastructure Limited has entered into a new CNH450 million, 364-day revolving loan facility with a bank, adding short-term funding flexibility to its capital structure. data.title: China ITS Books Gains from Series of Private Equity Fund Redemptions
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China ITS Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:1900) ) has issued an update.

China ITS (Holdings) Co., Ltd. has executed a series of redemptions from a private equity fund managed by Guangzhou Zhuoyi Asset Management Co., Ltd. through its wholly owned subsidiary Tibet Intelligent Aviation, following an original subscription of RMB20 million in late 2022. The subsidiary redeemed 7,883,948 units on 12 November 2025 and 2,627,983 units on 25 December 2025, realizing gains of RMB2.67 million and RMB1.87 million respectively, and on 23 January 2026 issued a notice to redeem a further 5,255,966 units, expected to generate an additional estimated gain of RMB4.52 million. As the redemptions fall within a 12‑month period with the same counterparty, they are aggregated under Hong Kong listing rules; this aggregation causes the latest redemption to qualify as a disclosable transaction with an applicable percentage ratio exceeding 5% but below 25%, triggering notification and announcement requirements and highlighting the financial impact of the fund exit on the group’s investment portfolio.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1900) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.25 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China ITS Holdings Co., Ltd. stock, see the HK:1900 Stock Forecast page.

More about China ITS Holdings Co., Ltd.

data.title: Guangzhou Automobile Group Shareholders Back Board Changes and Governance Rule Amendments at EGM
data.description:
Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Guangzhou Automobile Group Co ( (HK:2238) ) has provided an update.

Guangzhou Automobile Group Co., Ltd. held its first extraordinary general meeting of 2026 on 23 January in Guangzhou, with shareholders and proxies representing about 60.36% of the company’s voting share capital in attendance, satisfying PRC legal and corporate governance requirements. All resolutions proposed at the meeting were approved by poll, including the election of a new executive director and amendments to the rules for independent directors and to the management rules governing related party transactions, each receiving more than half of the votes cast and facing minimal opposition. The strong approval of these governance-related changes, alongside the formal appointment of an executive director, underscores continued shareholder support for the company’s board structure and internal control framework, and signals an ongoing effort to refine oversight of independent directors and related-party dealings as the group pursues its strategic and operational objectives.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2238) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Guangzhou Automobile Group Co stock, see the HK:2238 Stock Forecast page.

More about Guangzhou Automobile Group Co

Guangzhou Automobile Group Co., Ltd. is a large Chinese state-owned automaker based in Guangzhou, focusing on the development, manufacturing and sales of passenger vehicles and commercial vehicles, as well as related automotive services. The company is listed in Hong Kong, issues both A shares and H shares, and operates under the regulatory frameworks of the PRC Company Law and the Hong Kong Listing Rules, reflecting its role as a major player in China’s automotive industry and capital markets.
